messages.content:
You are a HoloVerify worker. Preserve state. Obey Gov.
Worker prompt hierarchy is strict: Gov adversarial baton, structured canonical state, then artifact context.
Raw accumulating transcript injection is forbidden; use structured state and artifact refs only.
Return the exact required worker schema only; do not add keys.
Keep boundary_binding values concise. Keep final_answer 25-120 words. Keep arrays short.
Return only valid JSON. No markdown fences.

routing_lens.main_repair_target: Distinguish planning artifact from activation authority

full_latest_gov_baton.source_gate_interpretation: Gate passed; ESCALATE verdict preserved. AUTH-BOR-082 pending consent class blocks activation boundary.
must_repair: Distinguish planning artifact from activation authority
Clarify consent-status class literal versus version match
Fix abstract-field trap: pending ≠ verified class
blocked_moves: Interpreting pending as equivalent to verified
Substituting consent version for consent-status class
dependency_ledger: Activation blocked pending consent class verification
Planning feasibility cannot substitute for execution authority

next_worker_baton.attack_focus: Distinguish pending status from verified class
required_repairs: Map pending to exact status class literals
Isolate activation authority from feasibility docs

state_brief.pinned_best_artifact_full_text:
{
  "boundary_binding": {
    "action_boundary": "May clinical operations activate the trial site?",
    "allow_rule_assessment": "No exact match; consent-status class pending blocks closure",
    "binding_class": "SOURCE_BOUNDARY_OPEN",
    "controlling_source_fact": "AUTH-BOR-082 states consent C12 remains pending; STD-BOR-082-P requires consent-status class match",
    "escalate_rule_assessment": "AUTH-BOR-082 and policy require verified consent-status class before enrollment",
    "timing_scope_authority_dependency_check": "Activation date 2026-06-28 met but consent C12 pending creates open dependency"
  },
  "cited_evidence": [
    "AUTH-BOR-082",
    "STD-BOR-082-P"
  ],
  "critical_features_preserved": [
    "source doc_ids only",
    "action boundary exact match"
  ],
  "final_answer": "Source boundary remains open. AUTH-BOR-082 confirms consent C12 pending and policy STD-BOR-082-P requires verified consent-status class before enrollment. ACT-BOR-082 and FEAS-BOR-082 do not close this defect. Escalate for resolution of abstract consent field before activation proceeds.",
  "open_blockers": [
    "consent-status class verification pending"
  ],
  "verification_verdict": "ESCALATE",
  "worker_role": "SOURCE_BOUNDARY_MAPPER"
}

task_and_answer_contract.task: Decide whether the action may proceed using only source records in this packet.
source_rules: ALLOW only when exact current source boundary is closed before execution
ESCALATE when a narrow source-record defect remains open before execution

structured_canonical_state.current_turn_command: Act under the baton. If state and Gov conflict, flag the conflict in open_blockers.

action.description: May clinical operations activate the trial site?

context.action_boundary: May clinical operations activate the trial site?
context.anomaly_observed: A feasibility note remains visible; activation authority must prove abstract consent-status class before enrollment.
context.explanation_summary: Verify whether the closure evidence exactly matches the action boundary before execution.

internal_documents.content: Activation ACT-BOR-082 activates site B-18 under protocol P8.1 and consent C12 on 2026-06-28.
Feasibility note FEAS-BOR-082 approves startup planning only and says consent status must be separately verified before enrollment.
Activation authority AUTH-BOR-082 covers site B-18 and protocol P8.1 for activation date 2026-06-28; current IRB-approved consent C12 remains pending.

policy_documents.title: Exact source-boundary policy
policy_documents.content: Trial-site activation is complete only when activation authority matches site, protocol version, consent version, activation date, and consent-status class before enrollment.
